description: Authoritative TS SDK public-surface inventory. Machine-readable, test-validated. Inventory is the source-of-truth for stability levels and change discipline; 007-CLIENT_SDK_GUIDE.md must stay aligned.

changeDisciplineValues.stable-deprecation-first: Breaking changes require a deprecation period. Removal only after at least one minor release with the deprecated API still available and a migration note in the migration ledger.
changeDisciplineValues.provisional-migration-required: Breaking changes are allowed but must be accompanied by a migration note in the migration ledger and a review-visible justification.
changeDisciplineValues.experimental-fast-break: Breaking changes are expected and may happen without prior deprecation. A brief note in the migration ledger is recommended but not enforced by the gate.

subpaths.note: Owns the current shared cancellation/resource-release baseline, the formal foundation-layer unified input-source helpers, the structured-observation baseline, the shared authenticated-principal baseline, and the dual-layer error bridge: DisposableTrait, CancellationTokenTrait, CancellationTokenSourceTrait, createCancellationTokenSource(), and createLinkedCancellationToken() formalize cooperative cancellation plus owner-side release via dispose(); timer(), interval(), scheduleAt(), fromEventPattern(), fromSignal(), and fromPromise() are the current scheduler/input primitives above raw runtime capabilities; TraceEvent, TraceEventSinkTrait, createTraceTimelineStore() / TraceTimelineStore, createOperationTracer(), and OperationTraceEventType now formalize the machine-readable trace sink plus operation-correlation story; AuthenticatedPrincipal, normalizeAuthenticatedPrincipal(), normalizeAuthenticatedPrincipalWire(), and projectAuthenticatedPrincipal() now formalize the shared human-principal contract and host-facing projection baseline consumed by session/token-set families; ErrorPresentationDescriptor / ErrorPresentationTone / readErrorPresentationDescriptor() remain the host-facing presentation and recovery bridge built on runtime errors.

Owns the low-level persistence contracts used by browser login/callback flows, including the RecordStore atomic take capability and the keyed ephemeral flow-store helpers that formalise single-consume redirect state.

Owns browser-native interop for the foundation cancellation baseline through both bridge directions: createAbortSignalBridge(token) for fetch-facing consumers and createCancellationTokenFromAbortSignal(signal) for browser/framework consumers that receive AbortSignal first. It also owns the browser-layer unified input-source helpers fromAbortSignal(), fromStorageEvent(), and fromVisibilityChange(), plus higher-level lifecycle helpers such as createCrossTabSync() and createVisibilityReconciler().

Canonical owner of shared auth requirement orchestration primitives (RequirementPlanner, RouteRequirementOrchestrator). Moved from @securitydept/token-set-context-client/orchestration in iteration 102. Kind is an opaque string; each auth-context defines its own named constants.

Foundation event-stream contracts and RxJS-backed operator facade used by auth lifecycle telemetry. Exposes SecurityDept traits rather than requiring family packages to make RxJS their primary public contract.

Iteration 110 — raw Web router/security baseline. Navigation API first with History API + popstate fallback. Pre-commit intercept wires PlannerHost requirement evaluation into both backends so non-framework (Web Components / Lit / vanilla) adopters have a canonical router/security path aligned with Angular and TanStack Router.

React entry keeps the layering explicit: BasicAuthContextProvider({ config }) takes auth-context config only, while the provider itself is host glue and does not introduce a separate runtime capability layer.

docsAnchor.heading: React entry: dedicated adapter packages own Provider and hook wiring

subpaths.note: Iteration 137 promotes framework-neutral browser-shell convenience into core: rememberPostAuthRedirect(), clearPostAuthRedirect(), resolveLoginUrl(), and logoutAndClearPendingLoginRedirect(). Iteration 142 aligns the user-info principal contract to the shared AuthenticatedPrincipal baseline from @securitydept/client, so stable subject/issuer/claims semantics no longer live in a narrower session-only owner.

React entry keeps auth-context config separate from runtime capabilities: SessionContextProvider({ config, transport, sessionStore }) treats config as session-context policy while transport/sessionStore remain capability wiring and the provider remains host glue.

docsAnchor.heading: React entry: dedicated adapter packages own Provider and hook wiring

subpaths.note: Owns token-set backend-mode route/client contracts. Iteration 142 keeps backend user-info transport as a family DTO but aligns its semantic authenticated-principal parsing/output to the shared AuthenticatedPrincipal baseline from @securitydept/client instead of a parallel family-local owner.

Includes the config projection source contract plus the current browser materialization owner: resolveConfigProjection(), networkConfigSource(), ConfigProjectionSourceKind, ClientReadinessState, createFrontendOidcModeBrowserClient(), resolveFrontendOidcModePersistentStateKey(), and resolveFrontendOidcModeBrowserStorageKey(). Iteration 108 added async clientFactory support. Iteration 120 tightened popup semantics so popupLogin() uses popupCallbackUrl as the real OAuth redirect_uri, supports an explicit postAuthRedirectUri for the opener, and is now proven by the apps/webui reference host via popup relay plus cross-tab lifecycle browser e2e. Iteration 121 adds FrontendOidcModeTraceEventType so popup / callback / refresh / user-info structured trace taxonomy is shared and adopter-visible rather than app-local. Iteration 122 adds describeFrontendOidcModeCallbackError() so callback-specific host-facing presentation/recovery mapping stays under the family owner while building on the shared client error presentation descriptor contract. Iteration 139 formalizes capability-first layering on the browser path: projection endpoint + redirectUri are bootstrap input, the resolved projection is auth-context config, runtime capability wiring stays explicit, and the reference app no longer owns fetch/parse/materialization assembly. Iteration 142 aligns frontend-mode user-info and metadata principal construction to the shared AuthenticatedPrincipal baseline and leaves app-facing display projection to shared helper-backed host code.

Token-set-specific orchestration layer: auth-transport, base-client, controller, persistence, token-ops, types. RequirementPlanner and RouteRequirementOrchestrator have been migrated to @securitydept/client/auth-coordination (iteration 102). Iteration 142 aligns AuthPrincipal to the shared AuthenticatedPrincipal baseline while keeping resource-token facts and substrate ownership outside this human-principal contract.

Iteration 110 + 116 review-2 — framework-neutral multi-client token-set auth registry core. Owns keyed storage, readiness tracking (not_initialized → initializing → ready | failed), priority=primary|lazy lifecycle, preload / whenReady / idleWarmup verbs, multi-axis discrimination (urlPatterns, callbackPath, requirementKind, providerFamily), and the shared managed OIDC client contracts (`OidcModeClient`, `OidcCallbackClient`). Angular and React adapters now wrap this shared contract owner thinly instead of redefining it locally.

Token-set-specific raw web route helper that creates AuthGuardClientOption entries whose unauthenticated handler first calls ensureAuthForResource({ source: raw_web_router, forceRefreshWhenDue: true }) before redirect/block fallback.

Owns focused test observation helpers for the current browser/SDK baseline: FakeClock, FakeScheduler, FakeTransport, and InMemoryTraceCollector. Iteration 141 adds operation-level trace observation through ofOperation(), operationLifecycle(), and assertOperationLifecycle() so tests do not need ad hoc operationId filters.
Angular entry keeps provideBasicAuthContext({ config }) as auth-context config only; Angular DI owns host registration, while the service facade stays a thin wrapper over core zone/redirect helpers.

docsAnchor.heading: Angular entry: thin DI wrappers preserve canonical owner boundaries

subpaths.note: Iteration 137 aligns Angular with the canonical session browser-shell path from core and keeps low-level access on SessionContextService.client instead of duplicating core methods on the Angular facade. The layering stays explicit: SessionContextClient owns auth-context config, transport remains runtime capability wiring, and Angular DI owns host registration.

docsAnchor.heading: Angular entry: thin DI wrappers preserve canonical owner boundaries

subpaths.note: Iteration 110 + 116 + 119 + 120 + 122 — React 19 productization, canonical keyed consumer-path closeout, browser-owned callback failure-path productization, frontend-mode popup/cross-tab host authority, and shared callback presentation descriptors. Adds TokenSetAuthProvider, useTokenSetAuthRegistry / useTokenSetAuthService / useTokenSetAuthState / useTokenSetAccessToken, useTokenSetCallbackResume, CallbackResumeErrorDetails, readCallbackResumeErrorDetails, TokenSetCallbackComponent (with a retained TokenSetCallbackOutlet compatibility alias), and the SDK-owned lower-level keyed accessor useTokenSetBackendOidcClient(clientKey) on top of the framework-neutral registry core (@securitydept/token-set-context-client/registry). In the current layering story, TokenSetAuthProvider owns host registration into the shared registry lifecycle; individual client entries still own auth-context config plus runtime capability composition. CallbackResumeErrorDetails now also carries a shared host-facing presentation descriptor so browser-owned callback hosts can render stable recovery guidance directly, and the canonical reference host proves that shared descriptor surface across callback, popup, and backend browser actions through apps/webui tests and browser e2e.

Iteration 115 + 116 — canonical token-set React Query consumer surface for groups / entries read and write workflows (not a separate package). @tanstack/react-query is an optional peer dependency. Auth lifecycle ownership still stays in the token-set / client-react family; the subpath owns query-key extensions, token-set management entity contracts, post-mutation invalidation for the canonical groups / entries flows, and the adopter-facing canonical path is now keyed-only (`clientKey`) instead of requiring explicit `client` passing.

Token-set-specific TanStack Router beforeLoad helper that wraps @securitydept/client-react/tanstack-router and calls registry.ensureAuthForResource({ source: tanstack_before_load, forceRefreshWhenDue: true }) for each route requirement before redirect/block fallback.

Root export owns TokenSetAuthRegistry DI service, multi-client integration, bearer interceptor (BearerInterceptorOptions { strictUrlMatch?: boolean } + TOKEN_SET_BEARER_INTERCEPTOR_OPTIONS InjectionToken; provideTokenSetBearerInterceptor(options?) and createTokenSetBearerInterceptor(registry, options?) accept the same options object — strictUrlMatch: true bounds bearer injection to URLs matching registered urlPatterns), callback resume, and the canonical Angular route-security builders. secureRouteRoot() / secureRoute() are the adopter-facing Angular Router path: child routes declare serializable requirement metadata only, root registers non-serializable runtime policy (planner hosts, requirementPolicies, handlers), and both canActivate + canActivateChild evaluate the full route chain in one pass. createTokenSetRouteAggregationGuard() remains the lower-level route-tree primitive. createTokenSetAuthGuard() has been removed from the package. Signal bridge utilities (bridgeToAngularSignal, signalToObservable) live in @securitydept/client-angular.

React planner-host integration: AuthPlannerHostProvider, useAuthPlannerHost, AuthRequirementsClientSetProvider, useEffectiveClientSet. Provides React Context glue for the shared PlannerHost contract from @securitydept/client/auth-coordination. Canonical import: @securitydept/client-react.

Canonical owner of TanStack React Router route-security contract, aligned with Angular's @securitydept/client-angular route metadata contract. Canonical adopter-facing entry (Iteration 107 Review1 fix): createSecureBeforeLoad() — a beforeLoad-compatible factory that wires root-level runtime policy into TanStack Router execution semantics (throws redirect or RouteSecurityBlockedError). Child routes use withTanStackRouteRequirements() for serializable staticData declaration only. Lower-level building blocks: extractTanStackRouteRequirements (full-route aggregation with merge/replace/inherit), createTanStackRouteSecurityPolicy (headless evaluator), projectTanStackRouteMatches, createTanStackRouteActivator.

Canonical owner of Angular framework bridge utilities. Provides: AuthRouteAdapter (projection from ActivatedRouteSnapshot to RouteMatchNode[]), PlannerHost DI wiring (provideAuthPlannerHost, AUTH_PLANNER_HOST, injectPlannerHost), route-metadata helpers (withRouteRequirements, extractFullRouteRequirements, ROUTE_REQUIREMENTS_DATA_KEY, ROUTE_REQUIREMENTS_COMPOSITION_DATA_KEY, resolveEffectiveRequirements), signal/Observable bridge utilities (bridgeToAngularSignal, signalToObservable — generic, not token-set-specific), and provideRouteScopedRequirements (lower-level non-router DI-scope helper). ReadableSignalTrait from @securitydept/client is the canonical signal contract.
